updates.title: ITR filing 2026: Employer chose new tax regime by default, can you switch to old regime and claim deductions now?
updates.summary: Salaried taxpayers can choose between old and new tax regimes until the ITR deadline. Employers will apply the new regime if no preference is communicated, but the final choice rests with the taxpayer during ITR filing. Details here.

updates.title: Mutual fund distributors seek tax relief after Sebi’s expense ratio overhaul
updates.summary: Distributors have urged the Association of Mutual Funds in India to adopt the reverse charge mechanism to offset income losses stemming from new expense ratio regulations. Many small distributors, particularly those exempt from GST, are significantly affected by the changes.
updates.user_impact: May affect your mutual fund investments or SIPs

updates.title: EPF vs SIP: Which Gets You To Rs 1 Crore Faster?
updates.summary: For investors focused solely on building a Rs 1 crore corpus in the shortest possible time, SIPs in diversified equity mutual funds generally hold the advantage, driven by the long-term return potential of the stock market.

updates.title: Use the 3/20/30/40 rule for home loans to buy a house without straining your finances
updates.summary: Avoid costly borrowing mistakes with the 3/20/30/40 rule for home loans as a guide to efficient home loan planning. This simple framework helps homebuyers manage EMIs, reduce debt burden and maintain long-term financial stability. It is prudent to consult a financial advisor while planning a loan.&nbsp;

updates.title: Resettled in India? Here's how Form 40 can help defer tax on overseas retirement savings until withdrawal
updates.summary: NRIs who become tax residents in India can defer tax on foreign retirement accounts until withdrawal by filing Form 40. This applies to accounts in the US, UK, Canada, and Australia, and offers relief from accrual taxation. Details here.

updates.title: Do FPIs still drive Dalal Street? Here's what the data shows about domestic investors changing market dynamics
updates.summary: Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) influence Indian markets but their control has diminished. Increased domestic participation has reduced reliance on foreign capital, allowing the market to remain resilient even during significant FPI outflows in recent years.

updates.title: ₹10 lakh lump sum vs ₹10,000 SIP for 100 months - which built a bigger corpus?
updates.summary: Investors often compare lump sum investing and SIPs for wealth growth. A lump sum involves a single, large investment, while a SIP allows regular, smaller contributions. Lump sum may be ideal for large sums, while SIPs benefit those who invest gradually from salaries.
